fdny.q: What does the F-01 Certificate of Fitness allow you to do?
choices: Drive FDNY fire trucks
Act as an impairment fire guard and maintain fire watch
Install sprinkler systems
Inspect gas lines
fdny.exp: The F-01 C of F authorizes the holder to act as an impairment fire guard and maintain fire watch responsibilities.

fdny.q: Where is the F-01 Certificate of Fitness valid?
choices: Only in Manhattan
Only in the building where you passed the exam
Citywide — any building in NYC
Only in buildings taller than 10 floors
fdny.exp: The F-01 is a CITYWIDE certificate. The fire guard may perform duties at different buildings and occupancies across all five boroughs.

fdny.q: How often must a fire guard patrol the affected area when a fire protection system is out of service?
choices: Every 30 minutes
Every 2 hours
At least once every hour
Twice per shift
fdny.exp: A fire guard must be available to patrol all areas in which the fire protection system is out of service at least once every hour.

fdny.q: What is the maximum floor area a SINGLE fire guard may patrol?
choices: 25,000 sq ft
50,000 sq ft
75,000 sq ft
fdny.exp: No individual fire guard shall patrol more than 50,000 square feet of building floor area.

fdny.q: What is the maximum height from the floor that the TOP of a portable fire extinguisher may be mounted?
choices: 3 feet
4 feet
5 feet
6 feet
fdny.exp: All fire extinguishers must be mounted so that the TOP of the extinguisher is NOT MORE THAN 5 feet above the floor.

fdny.q: What is the minimum height from the floor that the BOTTOM of a portable fire extinguisher must be?
choices: At floor level (0 inches)
At least 2 inches
At least 4 inches
At least 12 inches
fdny.exp: The bottom of the fire extinguisher must be at LEAST 4 inches from the floor. Extinguishers are NOT allowed to be placed directly on the floor.

fdny.q: What does P.A.S.S. stand for when using a fire extinguisher?
choices: Push, Activate, Spray, Sweep
Pull, Aim, Squeeze, Sweep
Pull, Angle, Spray, Stop
Push, Aim, Squeeze, Start
fdny.exp: P.A.S.S. = Pull (the pin), Aim (at the base of the fire), Squeeze (the handle), Sweep (side to side).

fdny.q: Class A fires involve which type of material?
choices: Flammable liquids like gasoline
Electrical equipment
Ordinary combustibles like wood, paper, and cloth
Ignitable metals like magnesium
fdny.exp: Class A fires are caused by ORDINARY COMBUSTIBLE MATERIALS such as wood, paper, and cloth.

fdny.q: Class B fires involve which type of material?
choices: Wood, paper, and cloth
Flammable or combustible liquids and gases such as oil and gasoline
Electrical equipment
Ignitable metals
fdny.exp: Class B fires are caused by FLAMMABLE OR COMBUSTIBLE LIQUIDS AND GASES such as oil, gasoline, etc.

fdny.q: Class C fires involve which type of material?
choices: Cooking oils
Ordinary combustibles
Electrical equipment
Compressed gases
fdny.exp: Class C fires INVOLVE ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT. These fires must be fought with extinguishers that do not conduct electricity.

fdny.q: When should you call 911 in a fire emergency?
choices: Only after you have tried to put out the fire
Only if the fire is on your floor
Immediately — there must be NO delays in making a notification
After notifying the building owner first
fdny.exp: If a fire guard becomes aware of a fire, he/she must IMMEDIATELY call 911. There must be NO DELAYS in making a notification.

fdny.q: During a fire, which floors must be evacuated FIRST?
choices: The basement and ground floor
The top two floors
The fire floor and the floor ABOVE it
All floors simultaneously
fdny.exp: Occupants on the FIRE FLOOR and the FLOOR ABOVE are in the greatest danger and must be evacuated first.

fdny.q: Should building occupants use elevators during a fire?
choices: Yes, elevators are the fastest way out
Only if they are on floors above the 5th floor
No — occupants should avoid elevators and use stairwells
Only if the fire is in the basement
fdny.exp: The fire guard should tell occupants to AVOID ELEVATORS and direct them to use stairwells to exit.

fdny.q: How long must fire watch inspection records be kept on the premises?
choices: 6 months
1 year
At least 3 years
Until the Certificate of Fitness expires
fdny.exp: A record must be kept on the premises for AT LEAST 3 YEARS. It must also be maintained for at least 48 hours after the fire watch has finished.

fdny.q: How many manual fire alarm pull stations are required per floor in a non-residential building?
choices: At least 2
At least 1
At least 3
It depends on floor size only
fdny.exp: There must be AT LEAST 1 MANUAL FIRE ALARM STATION ON EACH FLOOR of a building (except residential buildings).

fdny.q: How often must portable fire extinguishers be given a 'quick check'?
choices: Daily
Weekly
Monthly
Every 6 months
fdny.exp: Portable fire extinguishers are required to be checked MONTHLY. This is called a 'quick check'.

fdny.q: Who must conduct the ANNUAL inspection of portable fire extinguishers?
choices: The fire guard holding the F-01
Any building employee
A W-96 Certificate of Fitness holder from an FDNY-approved company
An FDNY firefighter
fdny.exp: At least annually, all PFEs must be checked by a W-96 Certificate of Fitness holder from an FDNY-APPROVED COMPANY.

fdny.q: You are a fire guard at a building in BROOKLYN where the sprinkler system has gone out of service. What phone number do you call to notify the FDNY (non-emergency impairment notification)?
fdny.exp: Brooklyn non-emergency FDNY notification number is (718) 965-8300.

fdny.q: You are a fire guard at a building in MANHATTAN where the standpipe system is out of service. What non-emergency number do you call to notify the FDNY?
fdny.exp: Manhattan non-emergency FDNY notification number is (212) 570-4300.

fdny.q: You are a fire guard at a building in the BRONX where the fire alarm system is out of service. What non-emergency number do you use to notify the FDNY?
fdny.exp: Bronx non-emergency FDNY notification number is (718) 430-0200.

fdny.q: Under which circumstance must the FDNY be notified that a SPRINKLER SYSTEM is out of service?
choices: Only if it has been out of service for more than 24 hours
If repairs cannot be completed and the system restored within 8 hours
Only if there is an active fire
Only if multiple buildings are affected
fdny.exp: FDNY must be notified if a sprinkler system cannot be restored within 8 HOURS, or if it is out of service on more than one floor, or if another fire protection system is also out of service.

fdny.q: When MUST the FDNY be notified about a standpipe system being out of service?
choices: Only if it will be out of service for more than 8 hours
Only if it affects more than one floor
WHENEVER a standpipe system is or will be out of service, for ANY period of time
Only on planned outages
fdny.exp: Notification must be made to the department WHENEVER a standpipe system is or will be out of service for ANY PERIOD OF TIME — no minimum time threshold.

fdny.q: During the first 4 hours of an UNPLANNED out-of-service condition affecting 40,000 sq ft, who may perform fire watch duties?
choices: Only an F-01 Certificate of Fitness holder
The impairment coordinator OR a trained and knowledgeable person designated by the building owner
Any building employee
The building super
fdny.exp: For the FIRST 4 HOURS of an unplanned or planned out-of-service condition when the affected area does NOT exceed 50,000 sq ft, the impairment coordinator OR a trained/knowledgeable person designated by the owner may perform fire watch.

fdny.q: After 4 hours of an out-of-service condition, who MUST conduct fire watch patrols?
choices: Any trained building employee
The building owner
Only fire guards holding the F-01 Certificate of Fitness
Any security guard
fdny.exp: After 4 hours of an out-of-service condition, patrols can ONLY be conducted by fire guards holding the F-01 CERTIFICATE OF FITNESS.

fdny.q: Where must manual fire alarm pull stations be located relative to exit doorways?
choices: Within 10 feet of the exit doorway opening
Within 5 feet of the exit doorway opening
Within 15 feet of the exit doorway opening
Anywhere visible on the floor
fdny.exp: Pull stations must be located WITHIN 5 FEET FROM THE EXIT DOORWAY OPENING and must never be blocked.

fdny.q: At what minimum height from the floor must the HANDLE of a manual fire alarm pull station be?
choices: 2 feet
3 feet
4 feet
5 feet
fdny.exp: The handle of a pull station should be AT LEAST 4 FEET FROM THE FLOOR.

fdny.q: A double-action fire alarm pull station requires how many steps to activate?
choices: 1 step
2 steps
3 steps
No steps — it activates automatically
fdny.exp: DOUBLE-ACTION stations require 2 STEPS: first break/open/lift a cover, then operate the switch or lever.

fdny.q: After a fire alarm pull station is activated, where must the alarm be RESET?
choices: At the pull station itself using the reset button
At the main Fire Alarm Control Panel (FACP)
By calling 911
By removing the battery
fdny.exp: The alarm CANNOT be reset at the pull station. It must be reset at the MAIN FIRE ALARM CONTROL PANEL (FACP) after being advised by a Fire Department representative. Only an S-95 holder may reset the FACP.

fdny.q: What four items MUST be logged in the fire guard's daily inspection record?
choices: Name, time in, time out, temperature
Number of inspections, defects found, violations found, and the date/name/COF number/signature of the fire guard
Building address, floor area, number of exits, hazmat locations
Owner name, fire guard name, patrol route, fire extinguisher count
fdny.exp: The daily record must log: (a) number of inspections completed; (b) defects found; (c) violations found; (d) date, name, COF number, and signature of the fire guard.

fdny.q: The fire guard inspection record must be maintained for how long AFTER the fire watch has finished?
choices: 24 hours
48 hours
72 hours
1 week
fdny.exp: The record must be maintained for AT LEAST 48 HOURS after the fire watch has finished.

fdny.q: At a construction site, who must be designated as the Impairment Coordinator when an installed and approved fire protection system is out of service?
choices: The F-01 fire guard
The Construction Site Fire Safety Manager (S-56)
The building owner's lawyer
The head of the construction crew
fdny.exp: The CONSTRUCTION SITE FIRE SAFETY MANAGER (S-56) must be designated as the impairment coordinator at construction sites when an installed and approved fire protection system is out of service.

fdny.q: At a construction site, is hot work allowed when the STANDPIPE SYSTEM is out of service?
choices: Yes, as long as extra fire extinguishers are present
Yes, but only for 4 hours
No — hot work is NOT allowed when the standpipe system is out of service
Yes, with written FDNY approval
fdny.exp: When the standpipe system is out of service at a construction site, NO HOT WORK IS ALLOWED.

fdny.q: What is the length of the hologram strip on a legitimate FDNY PFE tag?
choices: 1 inch long by ¼ inch wide
3 inches long by ¼ inch wide
2 inches long by ½ inch wide
5 inches long by 1 inch wide
fdny.exp: A REAL hologram strip is 3 INCHES LONG BY ¼ INCH WIDE. Counterfeit tags will NOT have a high-quality silver hologram.

fdny.q: If you suspect a PFE tag is counterfeit, what should you do?
choices: Throw away the extinguisher immediately
Replace the tag yourself
Contact FDNY immediately by email: Tags.Decal@fdny.nyc.gov
Call the extinguisher company that serviced it
fdny.exp: If you suspect a PFE is counterfeit, contact FDNY immediately by email at Tags.Decal@fdny.nyc.gov.

fdny.q: During a fire guard patrol, if locks or chains are found on exit doors, what must happen?
choices: They can remain if they are padlocks
Report them to the FDNY immediately and wait
They must be removed immediately; the fire guard must report to the building owner
Lock and chain the door from the outside for safety
fdny.exp: Locks, bolts and chains CANNOT be installed on exit doors while the building is in use. If locks are seen, they must be REMOVED IMMEDIATELY and the fire guard must report to the building owner.

fdny.q: What must the minimum width of an exit aisle be?
choices: At least 2 feet wide
At least 3 feet wide
At least 4 feet wide
At least 5 feet wide
fdny.exp: The exit aisle is generally required to be AT LEAST 3 FEET WIDE.

fdny.q: A sprinkler system on floors 3, 5, and 7 of a Manhattan office building unexpectedly goes out of service at 9:00 AM on a Tuesday. Which TWO notification requirements are triggered?
choices: Only call 911 since it is an emergency
Notify FDNY because the system is out on MORE THAN ONE FLOOR; also because it is unlikely to be restored within 8 hours
Only notify the building owner
No notification is required until 8 hours have passed
fdny.exp: Two triggers apply: (1) the system is out of service on MORE THAN ONE FLOOR, and (2) if repairs cannot be completed within 8 hours. Either condition independently requires FDNY notification.

fdny.q: A building has a floor area of 120,000 square feet where the sprinkler system is impaired. How many F-01 fire guards are required MINIMUM to cover this area?
choices: 1 fire guard
2 fire guards
3 fire guards
4 fire guards
fdny.exp: No fire guard may patrol more than 50,000 sq ft. 120,000 ÷ 50,000 = 2.4, so a minimum of 3 fire guards are required.

fdny.q: Class D fires involve ignitable metals. Which of the following is a Class D metal listed in the F-01 study material?
choices: Iron and steel
Magnesium, titanium, and metallic sodium
Copper and bronze
Aluminum only
fdny.exp: Class D fires involve IGNITABLE METALS such as MAGNESIUM, TITANIUM, and METALLIC SODIUM, or metals combustible under certain conditions like calcium, zinc, and aluminum.

fdny.q: After a fire extinguisher has been USED (discharged) during a fire watch, what must happen before work can restart?
choices: The used extinguisher may remain if the fire is out
A report must be filed within 24 hours
A FULLY CHARGED REPLACEMENT is required before work can restart
The fire guard must complete additional training
fdny.exp: If the fire extinguisher has been used (discharged), a FULLY CHARGED REPLACEMENT IS REQUIRED BEFORE WORK CAN RESTART.

fdny.q: When charging MORE THAN FIVE personal mobility devices (e-bikes, scooters), what special requirement applies?
choices: They must be charged outdoors only
Charging must be done in a dedicated room with ventilation and a self-closing door
They must be charged one at a time
An FDNY permit is required
fdny.exp: When charging MORE THAN 5 personal mobility devices or their removable batteries, it must be in a DEDICATED ROOM WITH VENTILATION AND A SELF-CLOSING DOOR.

fdny.q: Can fire extinguishers be used to put out lithium-ion battery fires?
choices: Yes — CO₂ extinguishers work well
Yes — Class D extinguishers are effective
No — fire extinguishers DO NOT work on lithium-ion battery fires
Yes — dry chemical extinguishers are effective
fdny.exp: FIRE EXTINGUISHERS DO NOT WORK ON LITHIUM-ION BATTERY FIRES. In the event of a fire, leave and CLOSE THE DOOR, then call 911 from a safe location.

fdny.q: A lithium-ion battery fire appears to be extinguished. Is it safe to assume the fire is completely out?
choices: Yes, once the visible flame is gone the fire is out
No — lithium-ion batteries can unexpectedly RE-IGNITE minutes, hours, or even days after all visible fire is gone
Only check again after 30 minutes
Yes, if water was used to cool it
fdny.exp: Reignition is COMMON. Lithium-ion batteries can unexpectedly RE-IGNITE (without warning) MINUTES, HOURS, and even DAYS after all visible fire has been put out.

fdny.q: Before starting a fire watch patrol, the fire guard MUST have enough knowledge to identify which 5 things?
choices: Owner name, COF number, patrol time, building height, fire department phone
Entry routes, level of out-of-service condition, location of hazardous materials, location of fire extinguishers, means for making required notifications
Exit count, stairwell widths, elevator locations, sprinkler head count, hydrant locations
Building permits, fire code sections, insurance certificate, tenant list, emergency contact list
fdny.exp: The F-01 booklet lists exactly these 5 items a fire guard must know: (1) entry routes, (2) level of out-of-service, (3) location of hazardous materials, (4) location of fire extinguishers, (5) means for required notifications.

fdny.q: Which COF holder is responsible for conducting inspections and certifying sprinkler system compliance with NYC Fire Code?
choices: F-01 fire guard
S-95 Certificate holder
S-12 Certificate holder
W-96 Certificate holder
fdny.exp: An S-12 COF holder (Supervision of Citywide Sprinkler System) is responsible for conducting inspections and certifying system compliance with the NYC Fire Code.

fdny.q: Which COF holder is responsible for performing inspections and ensuring maintenance of a Fire Alarm System?
fdny.exp: An S-95 Certificate of Fitness holder is responsible for performing inspections and ensuring the maintenance of the Fire Alarm System.

fdny.q: What information must the impairment coordinator provide when calling to notify the FDNY of an out-of-service condition? (Select the MOST complete answer)
choices: Building address and type of system out of service
Name, building address, type of system, planned or unplanned, duration, floors affected, status of other systems, and COF holder name and number
Just the building address and borough
Building address and estimated repair time only
fdny.exp: The impairment coordinator must provide: name/contact info, building address, type of system, planned or unplanned, estimated duration, floors affected, status of other fire protection systems, and the name/COF number of the responsible COF holder.

fdny.q: According to the F-01 study material, what is the maximum quantity of aerosol products allowed in a retail display area at GROUND LEVEL?
choices: 5,000 pounds
10,000 pounds
24,000 pounds
50,000 pounds
fdny.exp: The maximum quantity of aerosol products in a retail display at GROUND LEVEL is 10,000 POUNDS. In a warehouse, it may be as much as 24,000 pounds per floor.

fdny.q: Does activating a manual fire alarm pull station automatically notify the FDNY?
choices: Yes, all pull stations are connected directly to the FDNY
Not necessarily — interior pull stations may NOT directly transmit a signal to the FDNY; a 911 call must ALWAYS be made
Only modern digital pull stations notify the FDNY
Only if the building has a central station monitoring contract
fdny.exp: The manual pull station MAY NOT directly transmit a signal to the FDNY. A PHONE CALL MUST ALWAYS BE MADE TO 911 OR THE FDNY DISPATCHER. Do NOT assume the FDNY has been called if you hear a fire alarm.

fdny.q: The fire guard record must be available for inspection by the Fire Department for at least how many years?
choices: 1 year
2 years
3 years
5 years
fdny.exp: Records must be kept on the premises for AT LEAST 3 YEARS and be available for inspection by any member of the FDNY.

fdny.q: The F-02 Certificate of Fitness is for fire guards working in which type of occupancy?
choices: Office buildings
Construction sites
Homeless shelters
Movie theaters
fdny.exp: The F-02 Certificate of Fitness is for FIRE GUARDS IN HOMELESS SHELTERS.

fdny.q: How often must an F-02 fire guard continuously patrol all areas of the shelter?
choices: Every 30 minutes
At least once per hour
Every 2 hours
Twice per shift
fdny.exp: F-02 fire guards must continuously patrol ALL AREAS of the shelter at LEAST ONCE AN HOUR.

fdny.q: Based on shelter fire case studies in the F-02 study material, what common activity caused two of the three featured shelter fires?
choices: Electrical wiring failures
Cooking left unattended / smoking materials (cigarettes/matches) left unattended
Gas leaks
Children playing with fire extinguishers
fdny.exp: Two of the three case-study fires were caused by unattended ignition sources: cooking left on a stove unattended (Red Roof Inn) and lit cigarettes igniting mattresses (Bronx shelter).

fdny.q: A key lesson from the Bronx shelter fire (December 2012) was that fire extinguishers were found empty and inoperable. How often should fire extinguishers be visually inspected to prevent this?
choices: Annually
Every 6 months
Monthly
Daily
fdny.exp: Fire extinguishers must be visually inspected MONTHLY to ensure they are in working order.

fdny.q: The F-02 Certificate of Fitness holder is supervised by which of the following?
choices: The FDNY fire marshal
A W-96 COF holder
F-80 or F-89/T-89 COF holder (or the Impairment Coordinator)
Any building security officer
fdny.exp: F-02 holders are generally supervised by an F-80 COF holder (Coordinator of Fire Safety & Alarm Systems in Shelters), an F-89/T-89 (Fire and Life Safety Director), or the Impairment Coordinator.

fdny.q: Before starting their patrols, what orientation must an F-02 fire guard receive?
choices: A tour of the entire city block
An orientation that includes the emergency preparedness plan for the shelter
A review of all NYC fire codes
A written test on fire extinguisher use
fdny.exp: The F-02 fire guard must receive an orientation from their supervisor upon the start of employment. This orientation MUST INCLUDE information to ensure they are familiar with the EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS PLAN for the shelter.

fdny.q: F-02 fire guards must maintain a record of what?
choices: Building maintenance requests
Records of patrols
Resident complaints
Food service logs
fdny.exp: F-02 fire guards are responsible for MAINTAINING A RECORD OF PATROLS.

fdny.q: If fire extinguishers are NOT provided in the fire guard's area of responsibility, what must the fire guard do?
choices: Request extinguishers from the FDNY
Carry a fire extinguisher with them
Leave the area until extinguishers are provided
Use a bucket of water
fdny.exp: If fire extinguishers are NOT PROVIDED in the fire guard's area, the fire guard must CARRY AN EXTINGUISHER WITH THEM.

fdny.q: When is an F-80 COF holder required to be on the premises of a shelter?
choices: Only during overnight hours
Only when the F-02 fire guard is not present
In any shelter with more than 15 persons that has a one-way voice communication fire alarm system
Only in shelters with more than 100 residents
fdny.exp: F-80 holders are required in buildings required to have a ONE-WAY VOICE COMMUNICATION system, that are operated or occupied for MORE THAN 15 PERSONS for a period of more than 30 days, including emergency shelters.

fdny.q: Which of the following is considered MISCONDUCT for a Certificate of Fitness holder?
choices: Arriving 5 minutes late to a shift
Cheating on an examination or presenting a fraudulent certificate
Using a cell phone for personal calls during a patrol
Requesting a pay increase
fdny.exp: Misconduct includes cheating on an examination, unauthorized changes to a certificate, possession of a fraudulent certificate, or impersonating another person.

fdny.q: What can happen to a Certificate of Fitness if the holder fails to promptly notify the Fire Department of a change in their address or work location?
choices: Nothing — address changes are not required
A $100 fine
Grounds for denial, non-renewal, suspension, or REVOCATION of the certificate
A temporary suspension only
fdny.exp: Failure to promptly notify the FDNY of any change in address or work location is grounds for DENIAL, NON-RENEWAL, SUSPENSION, OR REVOCATION of the certificate.

fdny.q: If an out-of-service condition occurs, can an F-02 fire guard continue their regular patrol of the affected area?
choices: No — they must immediately leave the area
No — only an F-01 holder may patrol
Yes — at the discretion of the supervisor, the F-02 holder may continue patrolling while paying special attention to the fire hazard
Yes — but only for the first 2 hours
fdny.exp: At the DISCRETION OF THE SUPERVISOR, the F-02 C of F holder who is performing regular fire watch patrols may continue to patrol the area surrounding the out-of-service condition while paying SPECIAL ATTENTION to the fire hazard.

fdny.q: What is TWO-WAY VOICE COMMUNICATION used for in a shelter fire emergency?
choices: To call 911 from a payphone
To allow staff on the fire floor to report conditions back to the Fire and Life Safety Director or F-80 holder at the fire command center
To broadcast an all-clear signal after a drill
To communicate with FDNY dispatch on a dedicated radio frequency
fdny.exp: Two-way voice communication allows staff members to report conditions of a fire emergency from the FIRE FLOOR back to the FLSD, F-80 holder, or Fire Drill Conductor at the fire command center using warden phones near exit stairways.

fdny.q: The Bronx shelter fires (December 2012) revealed that mattresses were improperly stored in the lobby and common areas. What was the consequence when children played with matches near them?
choices: The fires were quickly contained by the sprinkler system
Smoke and flames spread into the stairwell, injuring 4 adults and 2 children; the building lacked alarms, operable extinguishers, and working fire escapes
Only minor smoke damage occurred
The children were safely evacuated without injuries
fdny.exp: The Bronx fires spread into the stairwell and hallways. The building had NO building-wide alarms, NO hallway smoke detectors, EMPTY extinguishers, and BROKEN fire escapes — resulting in 4 adults and 2 children seriously injured.

fdny.q: According to the F-02 study material, approximately how many homeless people (including children) were sleeping in NYC's main municipal shelter system in September 2023?
choices: About 25,000
About 50,000
About 87,907
About 120,000
fdny.exp: As of September 2023, approximately 87,907 homeless people, including 31,510 homeless children, were sleeping each night in NYC's main municipal shelter system.

fdny.q: After immediately calling 911 about a fire in a shelter, what should an F-02 fire guard do next?
choices: Begin using a fire extinguisher immediately
Notify the impairment coordinator, FLSD, or other on-site responsible personnel — but ONLY AFTER calling 911
Wait for the FDNY to arrive before notifying anyone else
Block the stairwell to prevent panic
fdny.exp: After IMMEDIATELY calling 911, the fire guard must ALSO notify the impairment coordinator, FLSD, CSFSM or other responsible on-site personnel — but ONLY AFTER calling 911 first.

fdny.q: A shelter's Certificate of Occupancy lists the lawful occupancy as 20 persons, but on a given night only 8 people are staying there. Is an F-80 COF holder still required?
choices: No — only 8 people are present, so no F-80 is needed
Yes — the Certificate of Occupancy establishes the lawful occupancy as more than 15 persons, so an F-80 is required regardless of actual count
Only if the shelter has a fire alarm system
Only during nighttime hours
fdny.exp: The F-80 requirement is based on the CERTIFICATE OF OCCUPANCY, not the actual occupant count. If the CO establishes lawful occupancy as more than 15 persons, an F-80 is required EVEN IF fewer than 15 people are actually present.

fdny.q: In the NYC shelter fire (August 28, 2012), what CONTAINED the fire to a single apartment?
choices: A fire guard used a fire extinguisher
The building's SPRINKLER SYSTEM quickly extinguished the fire
FDNY arrived within 2 minutes
The tenant evacuated and closed the fire door
fdny.exp: The fire was QUICKLY EXTINGUISHED BY THE BUILDING'S SPRINKLER SYSTEM, confining the fire to a single apartment. The building had recently been fined for failure to provide sprinkler protection.

fdny.q: The F-03 Certificate of Fitness covers safety personnel at which type of occupancy?
choices: Homeless shelters
Buildings where fire protection systems are impaired
INDOOR places of assembly with a Place of Assembly Certificate of Operation
Temporary outdoor events only
fdny.exp: F-03 is a PREMISES-RELATED certification for occupancies with a PLACE OF ASSEMBLY CERTIFICATE OF OPERATION (PA) — like theaters, arenas, TV studios.

fdny.q: How many people gathering INDOORS triggers the requirement for at least one place of assembly safety personnel?
choices: 50 or more
75 or more
100 or more
200 or more
fdny.exp: The FDNY may require at least one place of assembly safety personnel at premises where the Certificate of Occupancy indicates that 75 OR MORE PEOPLE will gather INDOORS.

fdny.q: How many people gathering OUTDOORS triggers the requirement for at least one place of assembly safety personnel?
choices: 75 or more
100 or more
150 or more
200 or more
fdny.exp: For OUTDOOR gatherings, the threshold is 200 OR MORE PEOPLE.

fdny.q: What does the ESCAPE acronym stand for in the F-03 pre-event inspection checklist?
choices: Evaluate, Secure, Check, Assess, Patrol, Exit
Exits, Storage, Capacity, Aisles, Protection, Emergency exit lighting
Emergency, Safety, Compliance, Access, Plan, Equipment
Exit, Stairs, Corridor, Alarm, Patrol, Egress
fdny.exp: ESCAPE: E-Exits (unblocked/unlocked/marked), S-Storage (clean, no combustibles in corridors), C-Capacity (not exceeded), A-Aisles (free and clear), P-Protection (sprinklers/alarm/extinguishers), E-Emergency exit lighting.

fdny.q: During an assembly event, are people allowed to stand in an aisle?
choices: Yes, if they are only there briefly
Yes, as long as they leave when asked
No — people are NOT allowed to stand in an aisle
Yes, as long as the aisle is wider than 6 feet
fdny.exp: People are NOT ALLOWED TO STAND IN AN AISLE. Required aisles must be unobstructed at all times.

fdny.q: Which Assembly Group includes restaurants, nightclubs, bars, and cabarets?
choices: Assembly Group A-1
Assembly Group A-2
Assembly Group A-3
Assembly Group A-4
fdny.exp: ASSEMBLY GROUP A-2 includes assembly uses for FOOD AND/OR DRINK CONSUMPTION: banquet halls, cabarets, casinos, nightclubs, restaurants, taverns, and bars.

fdny.q: Which Assembly Group includes motion picture theaters, concert halls, and television studios with an audience?
choices: Assembly Group A-1
Assembly Group A-2
Assembly Group A-3
Assembly Group A-4
fdny.exp: ASSEMBLY GROUP A-1 includes assembly uses usually with FIXED SEATING for production and viewing of performing arts or MOTION PICTURES: movie theaters, concert halls, TV studios with an audience.

fdny.q: According to the F-03 study material, what is the most effective way to notify building occupants in case of a fire emergency?
choices: Shouting loudly
Calling 311
Activating the fire alarm pull station
Texting the building owner
fdny.exp: Activating the PULL STATION is the MOST EFFECTIVE WAY to notify building occupants in case of a fire emergency.

fdny.q: If you notice that your area of assembly is becoming overcrowded during an event, what is the CORRECT action?
choices: Decide on your own to stop people at the door
Inform your supervisor immediately and follow their instructions
Call 911 immediately
Ask patrons to leave voluntarily
fdny.exp: If overcrowding is noticed, you must INFORM YOUR SUPERVISOR IMMEDIATELY and FOLLOW THEIR INSTRUCTIONS.

fdny.q: The ESCAPE pre-event checklist requires that a capacity certificate placard be posted. What does this placard indicate?
choices: The building's fire rating
The maximum number of persons authorized in the space
The date of the last FDNY inspection
The names of all COF holders on duty
fdny.exp: The CAPACITY CERTIFICATE PLACARD is required to be POSTED and shows the MAXIMUM NUMBER OF PERSONS authorized to be in the space.

fdny.q: What is a key lesson from the Station Nightclub Fire (West Warwick, RI, 2003) that applies to place of assembly safety personnel?
choices: Always have at least 3 exits per floor
Most victims died rushing to the PRIMARY entrance — proper exit signing and directing occupants to ALL exits could have saved lives
Nightclubs should never host live music
Foam insulation is the safest material for acoustic panels
fdny.exp: Most victims at the Station Nightclub died at the PRIMARY ENTRANCE because of a logjam. The lesson is: proper exit SIGNS and safety personnel DIRECTING people to use exit routes other than the front door saves lives.

fdny.q: In a performing arts theater, when must the event coordinator make an audio announcement about exit locations?
choices: At the conclusion of the performance
No more than 10 minutes prior to the start of each performance
Only if the theater is more than 75% full
Only during fire drills
fdny.exp: The event coordinator must make an audio announcement NOT MORE THAN 10 MINUTES PRIOR TO THE START of each performance or program, informing occupants of exit locations.

fdny.q: Who must conduct the DAILY visual check in a performing arts or motion picture theater before each performance?
choices: The building owner
Any trained employee
The F-03 Certificate of Fitness holder
The S-95 COF holder
fdny.exp: The F-03 CERTIFICATE OF FITNESS HOLDER must conduct the DAILY VISUAL CHECKS in a performing arts or motion picture theater on any day the premises is to be used for a performance.

fdny.q: If an out-of-service fire alarm system is found in a theater where you hold an F-03, can you cover the required fire watch duties using your F-03 certificate?
choices: Yes, the F-03 covers all fire guard duties
Yes, but only for the first 4 hours
No — the F-03 certificate DOES NOT cover F-01 duties; an F-01 COF holder must be obtained
Yes, if you have held the F-03 for more than 2 years
fdny.exp: The F-03 CERTIFICATE WILL NOT COVER DUTIES FOR F-01. Your supervisor must arrange for a SEPARATE F-01 COF holder. Also, if you hold both F-01 and F-03, you CANNOT do both duties at the same time.

fdny.q: What should happen if a defect is discovered during the F-03 pre-event visual check that CANNOT be immediately resolved?
choices: Proceed with the event and monitor the situation
The event may be CANCELED; additional FDNY measures (watchline, extra fire guards) may be required
Document it and resolve it within 24 hours
Reduce the capacity by 10%
fdny.exp: If an issue cannot be resolved immediately, the EVENT MAY BE CANCELED. Additional fire guards, an FDNY watch line (fire company remains on scene), or other measures must occur before the event can begin.

fdny.q: In a performing arts theater, barriers separating standing audience members from passage areas must be at what height?
choices: At least 2 feet and not more than 3 feet above the floor
Not less than 3 feet and not more than 4 feet above the floor
At least 4 feet and not more than 5 feet above the floor
At least 5 feet and not more than 6 feet above the floor
fdny.exp: Barriers separating standing areas must be at a height of NOT LESS THAN 3 FEET AND NOT MORE THAN 4 FEET above the floor, supported by lightweight posts that do not obstruct emergency egress.

fdny.q: The Colectiv Nightclub fire in Bucharest (2015) killed 64 people. What combination of conditions caused the high death toll?
choices: Lack of smoke detectors and no staff on duty
Only one working egress door (31 inches wide), no fire suppression system, club authorized for 80 but overcrowded, and illegal pyrotechnics ignited flammable acoustic foam
Power outage and blocked emergency exits
Too few security guards and no medical team on site
fdny.exp: The Colectiv disaster combined: illegal pyrotechnics igniting flammable acoustic foam, ONLY ONE WORKING EXIT DOOR (31 inches wide causing a stampede), NO FIRE SUPPRESSION SYSTEM, and the club was AUTHORIZED FOR 80 SEATS but was overcrowded.

fdny.q: How long must all logbooks and records in a performing arts theater be kept on premises and available for FDNY inspection?
choices: 1 year
2 years
At least 3 years
5 years
fdny.exp: All logbooks and records (daily checks, standpipe/sprinkler inspection records, manual fire alarm box records, skylight/stage vent records) must be kept on the premises for a MINIMUM OF 3 YEARS.

fdny.q: How many questions are on the F-03 exam and what score is required to pass?
choices: 20 questions; 65% passing
25 questions; 70% passing
30 questions; 75% passing
25 questions; 80% passing
fdny.exp: The F-03 exam consists of 25 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS. A passing score of at least 70% (18/25) is required.

fdny.q: In a performing arts theater balcony, how many rows of persons are allowed to stand?
choices: Two rows
Three rows
One row only
No standing is permitted in balconies
fdny.exp: For BALCONIES, only ONE ROW OF PERSONS IS ALLOWED TO STAND.

fdny.q: The F-04 Certificate of Fitness covers safety personnel for which type of event?
choices: Permanent indoor theaters and arenas
Homeless shelter fire watches
TEMPORARY place of assembly events — festivals, outdoor concerts, public gatherings requiring a TPA permit
Buildings with impaired fire protection systems
fdny.exp: F-04 holders are responsible for TEMPORARY PLACE OF ASSEMBLY (TPA) EVENTS — festivals, outdoor concerts, or other temporary public gatherings. They work at DIFFERENT LOCATIONS, not a fixed premises.

fdny.q: For a TPA event, when must safety personnel ARRIVE at the premises?
choices: 30 minutes before the event
At least 1 hour before the event
When the event begins
2 hours before the event
fdny.exp: For TPA events, safety personnel are required to arrive at the premises AT LEAST 1 HOUR PRIOR TO THE EVENT and remain until all audience members leave.

fdny.q: Is the storage, handling, and use of portable Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) cylinders allowed at outdoor public gatherings?
choices: Yes, if stored in a designated area
Yes, if used only for cooking
No — CNG cylinders are PROHIBITED at outdoor public gatherings
Yes, for generators only
fdny.exp: The storage, handling, and use of portable CNG CYLINDERS ARE PROHIBITED AT OUTDOOR PUBLIC GATHERINGS.

fdny.q: Is it legal to store flammable liquids at outdoor public gatherings?
choices: Yes, in approved containers
Yes, up to 5 gallons
No — storage of flammable liquids is PROHIBITED at outdoor public gatherings
Yes, if used only for cooking
fdny.exp: It is ILLEGAL to store, handle, or use flammable liquids at outdoor public gatherings (except in listed generators or other approved devices). Storage of flammable liquids is PROHIBITED, and all fueling of generators must be conducted before the event opens to the public.

fdny.q: Can guests charge or store lithium-ion batteries at temporary outdoor events?
choices: Yes, in a designated charging area
Only if it is under a covered structure
No — guests are NOT PERMITTED to charge or store lithium-ion batteries at events
Only if event staff supervise the charging
fdny.exp: Guests are NOT PERMITTED to charge or store lithium-ion batteries at events.

fdny.q: Is pyrotechnic use at outdoor public gatherings allowed without a permit?
choices: Yes, for small sparklers and handheld devices
Yes, if no flammable materials are nearby
No — pyrotechnic use is STRICTLY PROHIBITED WITHOUT AN FDNY PERMIT
Yes, if the event is in a parking lot
fdny.exp: Pyrotechnic use is STRICTLY PROHIBITED WITHOUT A PERMIT FROM THE FDNY.

fdny.q: At a TPA event, what communication device must safety personnel posted at exits carry?
choices: A cell phone
A megaphone
A two-way radio (walkie-talkie)
A pager
fdny.exp: Every safety personnel posted at an exit must be equipped with a TWO-WAY RADIO COMMUNICATION (walkie-talkie) to communicate with persons manning a telephone to the Fire Department for an emergency.

fdny.q: At a TPA event, what device must safety personnel posted at entrances carry to monitor the occupant count?
choices: A walkie-talkie
A fire extinguisher
A device such as a scanner, hand-held counter, or ticket system to verify the occupancy count
A first-aid kit
fdny.exp: Every safety personnel posted at an event ENTRANCE must be equipped with a DEVICE (scanner, hand-held counter, ticket) to verify and monitor the OCCUPANCY COUNT.

fdny.q: Before a temporary place of assembly event, who must provide an approved site plan?
choices: The F-04 COF holder
The FDNY
The EVENT SPONSOR AND ANY PROMOTER (approved by NYC Department of Buildings)
The venue owner
fdny.exp: Prior to the TPA event, the SPONSOR AND ANY PROMOTER of the event must provide a SITE PLAN APPROVED BY THE NYC DEPARTMENT OF BUILDINGS.

fdny.q: What is required of temporary seating at outdoor assembly events?
choices: Temporary seats may be placed anywhere with enough space
Seats must be along walls only
Temporary seats must be SECURED TOGETHER (ganged) in an approved layout
Each seat must be bolted to the ground
fdny.exp: Temporary seating must be SECURED TOGETHER (GANGED) in an approved layout. Temporary, unsecured or un-ganged chairs may pose a great hazard in the event of an emergency.

fdny.q: When must generators and approved equipment be fueled at outdoor TPA events?
choices: During the event, in a designated area
Any time during the event
BEFORE the event is open to the public
Only by licensed fuel operators
fdny.exp: All fueling of generators and other approved devices, equipment and systems must be conducted BEFORE THE EVENT IS OPEN TO THE PUBLIC.

fdny.q: An applicant passes the F-03 exam. Can they obtain the F-04 Certificate of Fitness without taking the F-04 exam?
choices: No — the F-04 exam must be taken separately
Yes — by paying an additional $25 fee to obtain the F-04 without taking the F-04 exam
Yes — the F-03 automatically covers F-04 duties
No — both exams must be taken on the same day
fdny.exp: Applicants who PASS THE F-03 EXAM are allowed to pay an additional $25 FEE to obtain the F-04 Certificate of Fitness WITHOUT taking the F-04 exam.

fdny.q: At the Love Parade Stampede (Duisburg, Germany, 2010), 21 people died when 1.4 million attendees overcrowded a space estimated for 250,000. What are the TWO key lessons for TPA safety personnel?
choices: Always have medical teams and extra security guards
CROWD CONTROL is critical — exceeding capacity must be prevented; WIDE ROUTES are essential for evacuation; communication with EMS is vital
Outdoor events should require metal detectors and ID checks
Events should be cancelled if more than 500,000 attend
fdny.exp: Key lessons: CROWD CONTROL and not exceeding capacity; WIDE ROUTES for evacuation; COMMUNICATION WITH EMS; and having INCIDENT PLANS in emergencies.

fdny.q: How many questions are on the F-04 exam, and how much time is given to complete it?
choices: 25 questions; 38 minutes
20 questions; 30 minutes
15 questions; 25 minutes
30 questions; 45 minutes
fdny.exp: The F-04 exam consists of 20 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS with 30 MINUTES to complete. (The F-03 has 25 questions and 38 minutes.)

fdny.q: At the Ellis Park Stadium disaster (Johannesburg, 2001), 43 people were crushed to death when 30,000 extra fans entered an already-full 60,000-capacity stadium. What failure by security personnel contributed to this?
choices: The fire alarm system was disabled
Security FAILED TO CLEARLY IDENTIFY AND DESIGNATE AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITY; poor decision-making; no operational command center
Exits were locked from the outside
Pyrotechnics ignited in the stands
fdny.exp: The Ellis Park disaster highlighted: OVERCROWDING with serious consequences; failure to CLEARLY IDENTIFY AND DESIGNATE areas of responsibility; POOR DECISION-MAKING by security personnel; and NO OPERATIONAL COMMAND CENTER.

fdny.q: The F-07 Certificate of Fitness authorizes the holder to:
choices: Supervise fuel oil burner operations
Conduct fire and emergency staff training and drills in buildings that do NOT require a Fire Safety Director
Install and test fire alarm systems
Operate pressurized sprinkler systems
fdny.exp: The F-07 C of F is for Fire and Emergency Drill Conductors (FEDC). They conduct staff training and drills in occupancies that do NOT require an F-25/F-58 Fire Safety Director or F-80 Coordinator of Fire Safety and Alarm Systems.

fdny.q: An F-07 Certificate of Fitness is valid for how many years?
choices: 1 year
2 years
3 years
5 years
fdny.exp: The F-07/W-07 Certificate of Fitness is valid for 3 years. It must be renewed before expiration. Certificates expired more than 1 year past expiration date will not be renewed — a new exam is required.

fdny.q: The F-07 is a PREMISE-SPECIFIC certificate. The W-07 differs because:
choices: W-07 requires more training hours
W-07 holders can work at more than one location citywide
W-07 is only for residential buildings
W-07 is valid for 5 years instead of 3
fdny.exp: F-07 is site-specific — the holder works only at the address listed on the certificate. W-07 is CITYWIDE — the holder can conduct fire and emergency drills at multiple locations throughout New York City.

fdny.q: The Emergency Preparedness Plan (EPP) must be reviewed and updated at minimum:
choices: Every 5 years
Every 3 years
Once a year
Every 6 months
fdny.exp: The Emergency Preparedness Plan must be reviewed and updated at least once a year, and whenever there are changes in staff assignments, building use/occupancy, or design and arrangement of the premises.

fdny.q: Where must a copy of the Emergency Preparedness Plan be maintained?
choices: Only in the building manager's office
At the FDNY headquarters for reference
On the premises in an approved location — at the fire command station if available, or in a first responder box in the lobby secured by a citywide standard key
Filed with the city building department
fdny.exp: The EPP must be kept on premises at the fire command station if the building has one; otherwise in a first responder box, secured by a citywide standard key, in the building lobby. It must be available to FDNY representatives immediately upon request.

fdny.q: During a fire drill, which occupants are NOT required to participate in the physical evacuation?
choices: All occupants must physically evacuate — no exceptions
Occupants with physical disabilities or special needs who are part of a shelter-in-place or area of refuge plan per the EPP
Only supervisors and managers are exempt
Visitors are the only occupants exempt
fdny.exp: Occupants with disabilities or special needs who are designated for shelter-in-place, areas of refuge, or assisted evacuation per the Emergency Preparedness Plan are not required to physically evacuate during a drill.

fdny.q: The PASS technique for using a portable fire extinguisher stands for:
choices: Pull, Activate, Squeeze, Spray
Pull, Aim, Squeeze, Sweep
Press, Aim, Start, Stop
Point, Arm, Set, Spray
fdny.exp: PASS: Pull the pin → Aim the nozzle at the base of the fire → Squeeze the handle → Sweep side to side at the base of the fire. Always aim at the BASE of the fire, not the flames.

fdny.q: The F-07 exam consists of how many multiple-choice questions, and what is the passing score?
choices: 25 questions, 80% passing
40 questions, 70% passing
50 questions, 75% passing
35 questions, 70% passing
fdny.exp: The F-07 exam has 40 multiple-choice questions on a touchscreen computer monitor. You have 61 minutes. A passing score of at least 70% is required to receive the Certificate of Fitness.

fdny.q: The F-53 Certificate of Fitness is required when:
choices: A building has any type of public address system
A building has a fire alarm system integrated with a one-way voice communication system
All buildings with more than 6 floors
Any building with a sprinkler system
fdny.exp: The F-53 is required to supervise/operate a fire alarm system that is integrated with a one-way voice communication system. If the building has a PA system independent from the fire alarm, the F-53 is NOT required.

fdny.q: The Fire Alarm Control Panel (FACP) generates three types of signals. They are:
choices: Warning, Alert, and Emergency
Alarm, Supervisory, and Trouble
Fire, Smoke, and Gas
Red, Yellow, and Green
fdny.exp: The FACP generates three signal types: (1) ALARM — indicates a fire/emergency, (2) SUPERVISORY — indicates a problem with a protected system (e.g., sprinkler valve closed), (3) TROUBLE — indicates a problem with the fire alarm system itself.

fdny.q: When a fire alarm signal is activated, the F-53 holder should FIRST:
choices: Immediately silence the alarm
Press the acknowledge switch or button
Call 911 before doing anything else
Evacuate all building occupants
fdny.exp: The first action when any fire alarm signal is generated is to press the ACKNOWLEDGE switch or button. This silences the audible annunciation at the FACP while the signal is being investigated, and shows the FACP operator is aware.

fdny.q: The ALARM SILENCE switch on the FACP:
choices: Resets the entire fire alarm system
Silences the building's audible notification devices (horns/bells) while FDNY investigates, without resetting the system
Turns off the fire alarm system permanently
Only silences the alarm at the control panel, not throughout the building
fdny.exp: The alarm silence switch silences audible notification devices throughout the building while the alarm condition is being investigated. The system remains in alarm state — it does NOT reset the fire alarm system.

fdny.q: An alarm log book entry must include all of the following EXCEPT:
choices: Date and time of the alarm
Type and location of the alarm
Name and certificate number of the F-53 holder
The personal cell phone number of all building tenants
fdny.exp: Alarm log book entries must include: date and time, type of signal, location (floor/zone), cause if known, action taken, and name/certificate number of the F-53 holder. Tenant cell numbers are not a log book requirement.

fdny.q: The S-95 Certificate of Fitness is for:
choices: Installing new fire alarm systems
Supervising and monitoring existing fire alarm systems
Repairing broken fire alarm components
Inspecting buildings for fire code violations
fdny.exp: The S-95 is for personnel responsible for supervising, monitoring, and maintaining fire alarm systems. Holders with F-89/T-89/F-80/F-53 for the same premises are exempt from needing a separate S-95 for that premises.

fdny.q: A fire alarm system must have a secondary power source because:
choices: It is required for aesthetic reasons
In the event of primary power failure, the secondary source ensures the system continues to operate
The secondary source powers notification devices only
NYC law requires two identical systems
fdny.exp: Fire alarm systems must have both a primary power source (building electrical supply) and a secondary power source (batteries). If the primary power fails, the secondary source maintains the system in operation for a specified period.

fdny.q: A SUPERVISORY signal from the FACP most likely indicates:
choices: A fire has been detected
A smoke detector has activated
A supervised valve in the sprinkler system has been closed or moved from its normal position
A person has pulled a manual alarm station
fdny.exp: A supervisory signal indicates that a supervised system or component is not in its normal operating condition — for example, a sprinkler valve being closed, a pressure change, or a tamper switch activating. This is NOT an alarm — it requires investigation.

fdny.q: A TROUBLE signal on the FACP indicates:
choices: A fire has been confirmed
A problem or fault within the fire alarm system itself (wiring fault, power failure, device trouble)
A sprinkler has discharged
A carbon monoxide detector has activated
fdny.exp: A trouble signal indicates a problem with the fire alarm system itself: broken wiring, open circuit, ground fault, power failure, or device malfunction. It does NOT mean a fire — but it means the system may not be fully functional and needs immediate attention.

fdny.q: When a fire alarm system is taken out of service for maintenance, the S-95 holder must:
choices: Post a note on the FACP and wait
Notify the FDNY Central Station and arrange for compensatory measures (e.g., fire watch)
Turn off the system and resume normal operations
Contact the building tenants only
fdny.exp: When a fire alarm system is out of service (OOS), the responsible certificate holder must notify the Central Station and arrange for compensatory measures such as a fire watch, impairment coordinator notification, and other required actions per Fire Code.

fdny.q: The S-95 exam consists of how many questions?
choices: 25 questions
35 questions
50 questions
75 questions
fdny.exp: The S-95 exam consists of 35 multiple-choice questions on a touchscreen computer monitor. You have 53 minutes to complete the test. A passing score of at least 70% is required.

fdny.q: The S-12 Certificate of Fitness is for:
choices: Installing new sprinkler systems in buildings
Supervising and maintaining citywide sprinkler systems
Only supervising dry pipe sprinkler systems
Performing annual hydraulic calculations for sprinkler systems
fdny.exp: The S-12 is for persons responsible for supervising and maintaining sprinkler systems (citywide). The S-12 exam has 75 multiple-choice questions — significantly more than other FDNY exams — reflecting the breadth of sprinkler system knowledge required.

fdny.q: In a WET PIPE sprinkler system:
choices: Pipes are filled with pressurized air until a sprinkler activates, then water fills and discharges
Pipes are always filled with water under pressure — water discharges immediately when a sprinkler activates
Water fills the pipes only after a fire alarm confirms a fire
All sprinklers discharge simultaneously when the system is activated
fdny.exp: Wet pipe systems are the most common — the pipes are always filled with water under pressure. When a sprinkler head is activated by heat, water discharges immediately. This is the simplest and most reliable sprinkler system type.

fdny.q: A DRY PIPE sprinkler system is used in areas where:
choices: Water pressure is too high for a wet pipe system
The pipes could freeze (unheated spaces, parking garages, loading docks)
Only deluge protection is required
High-hazard occupancies require extra water volume
fdny.exp: Dry pipe systems contain pressurized air (or nitrogen) instead of water. When a sprinkler activates, the air escapes, the dry pipe valve opens, and water fills the pipes and discharges. Used where pipes could freeze in cold, unheated areas.

fdny.q: A sprinkler waterflow alarm indicates:
choices: The sprinkler system is being tested by a qualified person
Water is flowing through the sprinkler piping — possibly indicating a sprinkler has opened or a pipe has broken
A valve has been closed in the sprinkler system
The system needs to be recharged
fdny.exp: A waterflow alarm activates when water moves through the sprinkler system. This can indicate: a sprinkler has activated (fire condition), a pipe has broken, or the system is being tested. Waterflow alarms must be investigated immediately.

fdny.q: When a sprinkler system is Out of Service (OOS), the building owner must:
choices: Hang a sign on the main sprinkler valve and wait for repairs
Notify the FDNY, arrange for a fire watch, and implement compensatory measures as required
Simply note it in the building log book
Shut down the building until repairs are complete
fdny.exp: When a sprinkler system is OOS, the building owner must notify the FDNY (and the Central Station if there is one), arrange for a fire watch, post notices, and implement required compensatory measures. OOS time must be minimized.

fdny.q: A standpipe system is designed to:
choices: Automatically suppress fires without firefighter intervention
Provide water outlets (hose outlets/connections) throughout a building for manual firefighting by FDNY or building occupants
Replace the need for portable fire extinguishers
Monitor air quality throughout a building
fdny.exp: A standpipe system is a network of pipes, valves, and outlets that provides water for firefighting at various locations within a building. FDNY uses Class I and II standpipes; Class III standpipes serve both firefighters and trained building occupants.

fdny.q: A Class I standpipe system is intended for use by:
choices: Building occupants — small hoses for self-protection
FDNY firefighters only — 2.5-inch fire department hose connections
Both occupants and FDNY — combination system
Automatic suppression without firefighter use
fdny.exp: Class I standpipes provide 2.5-inch hose connections for use by FDNY firefighters. Class II provides 1.5-inch hose for building occupants. Class III combines both, providing outlets for FDNY (2.5") and occupants (1.5").

fdny.q: Hose outlet valves on a standpipe system should be inspected for:
choices: Rust and corrosion only
Proper position (normally closed), accessibility, absence of obstructions, and absence of leaks
Thread condition only — flow testing is not required
Visual inspection is not required if the system was recently installed
fdny.exp: Standpipe hose outlet valves must be regularly inspected for: proper position (closed), accessibility (not obstructed), no leaks, and proper pressure. Obstructions to standpipe access are a serious fire code violation.

fdny.q: A foam-water sprinkler system is typically used in:
choices: Residential high-rise buildings
Aircraft hangars, fuel storage areas, and other high-hazard flammable liquid occupancies
Office buildings and schools
Underground parking garages only
fdny.exp: Foam-water sprinkler systems discharge an aqueous film-forming foam (AFFF) and water mixture. They are used in high-hazard areas where flammable liquids are present: aircraft hangars, fuel loading facilities, vehicle repair areas with flammable liquids.

fdny.q: The foam concentrate in a foam-water system must be:
choices: Replaced every year regardless of condition
Tested periodically for quality and concentration to ensure it remains effective
Never changed — it lasts indefinitely
Mixed with water in the tank at all times
fdny.exp: Foam concentrate degrades over time. Periodic testing (typically every 3 years per NFPA standards) is required to ensure the foam retains its required concentration and fire suppression properties.

fdny.q: The G-44 Certificate of Fitness covers:
choices: Use of LPG/CNG in mobile cooking at outdoor events only
General storage and handling of LPG (Liquid Petroleum Gas) and CNG (Compressed Natural Gas)
Operation of fuel oil burners
Hot work operations using gas torches
fdny.exp: The G-44 C of F is for the general storage and handling of LPG (propane/butane) and CNG (compressed natural gas). G-44 is the foundational LPG certificate; other G-series certificates (G-22, G-23, G-40, etc.) cover specific uses.

fdny.q: LPG (Liquid Petroleum Gas, such as propane) is HEAVIER than air. This means:
choices: LPG rises to the ceiling when it leaks, where it is harmless
LPG settles at floor level and in low areas when it leaks, creating an invisible explosion hazard
LPG disperses evenly throughout a room
LPG is lighter than CNG and safer to store indoors
fdny.exp: LPG is heavier than air — it settles at floor level, accumulates in pits, basements, and low areas, creating an invisible explosion/fire hazard. This is why LPG storage requires special ventilation and why you check for leaks at floor level.

fdny.q: CNG (Compressed Natural Gas) differs from LPG because CNG is:
choices: Heavier than air and settles at floor level
Lighter than air and rises, dispersing upward when it leaks
More flammable than LPG at all concentrations
Stored as a liquid under pressure at room temperature
fdny.exp: CNG (mainly methane) is lighter than air — when it leaks, it rises and disperses upward. This is different from LPG (propane/butane) which settles. Understanding this distinction is critical for leak detection and safe storage.

fdny.q: To check for a gas leak, you should:
choices: Use a lighter or match to test for gas smell
Apply soapy water solution to connections and fittings — bubbles indicate a leak
Smell near all connections (no tools needed)
Open all windows and wait to see if the smell disappears
fdny.exp: To check for gas leaks: apply a soapy water solution to fittings, valves, and connections. Bubbles indicate a leak. NEVER use an open flame to test for leaks — this can cause explosion and fire.

fdny.q: LPG cylinders stored indoors must NOT exceed:
choices: One cylinder of any size
A total of 300 lbs of LPG water capacity per floor
A total of 1,000 lbs water capacity in any building
Any amount as long as cylinders are secured
fdny.exp: Indoor LPG storage is strictly limited. In most occupancies, no more than 300 lbs water capacity of LPG may be stored on any floor above grade. Specific limits vary by occupancy type per FDNY rules and Fire Code.

fdny.q: When moving compressed gas containers (cylinders), you should:
choices: Roll them on their bottom edge for short distances
Always use a hand truck or cylinder cart — never drag, slide, or roll them on their sides
Carry them horizontally to lower the center of gravity
Remove the regulator and cap before moving
fdny.exp: Compressed gas cylinders must be moved using a proper hand truck or cylinder cart. Never drag, slide, or roll cylinders — they can be damaged. Always keep the valve cap on when cylinders are not connected to equipment.

fdny.q: The G-60 Certificate of Fitness covers:
choices: Storage of LPG/CNG only
Use of flammable gases with oxygen, or LPG/CNG for hot work (torch) operations — welding, cutting, brazing, soldering
Supervision of fuel oil burners
Emergency drill coordination in buildings with gas systems
fdny.exp: The G-60 C of F is for hot work operations using flammable gases (with or without oxygen), including torch-applied roofing systems. All hot work operations must be personally performed by the C of F holder.

fdny.q: A FIRE WATCH after completing hot work operations is required for:
choices: 5 minutes after the work is done
30 minutes after the completion of all hot work operations
Only if combustible materials are present
Until the next business day
fdny.exp: After any hot work operations, a fire watch must be maintained for a minimum of 30 minutes after the completion of all hot work. This is because heated materials can smolder and ignite long after the torch is turned off.

fdny.q: A BACKFIRE during torch operation is:
choices: A brief flame pop back into the torch tip, followed by the flame going out or reigniting
An explosion of the gas cylinder
Flame traveling back through the hose to the gas source
Accidental ignition of surrounding combustible materials
fdny.exp: A backfire is a brief popping or snapping of the flame back into the torch tip, followed by the flame going out or reigniting. It is usually caused by: the tip touching the work, overheating of the tip, or incorrect tip size. It is LESS dangerous than a flashback.

fdny.q: A FLASHBACK during torch operation is more dangerous than a backfire because:
choices: It makes a louder noise
The flame travels back through the hose toward the gas source, risking hose/regulator/cylinder explosion
It uses more gas
It only occurs with LPG, not oxygen-fuel torches
fdny.exp: A flashback occurs when the flame travels back through the hose toward the gas source. This is extremely dangerous — it can cause the hose, regulator, or cylinder to explode. Turn off the gas immediately and do NOT restart until the equipment is inspected.

fdny.q: Hot work is PROHIBITED in which of the following areas?
choices: Outdoors on a construction site with a fire watch present
Within 35 feet of stored flammable liquids, flammable gases, or explosive materials without specific authorization
In a building under renovation where a permit has been obtained
On a rooftop during daylight hours
fdny.exp: Hot work is prohibited within 35 feet of flammable or combustible materials, storage areas, or explosive materials unless specific protective measures and authorization are in place. These are 'restricted hot work areas' that require additional precautions.

fdny.q: The P-99 Certificate of Fitness is for:
choices: Permanent fuel oil burner installations
Supervision of portable gasoline-powered equipment used indoors (generators, pressure washers, compressors)
Storage of portable LPG cylinders
Portable fire extinguisher maintenance
fdny.exp: The P-99 C of F is for persons supervising the use of portable fueled equipment (gasoline-powered generators, compressors, pressure washers, etc.) in indoor locations. Such equipment produces carbon monoxide and is strictly regulated.

fdny.q: Gasoline-powered generators run indoors create which specific deadly hazard?
choices: Excessive noise above OSHA limits
Carbon monoxide (CO) — a colorless, odorless, deadly gas
Sparks that can ignite combustibles
Oil spills that create slip hazards
fdny.exp: Gasoline engines produce carbon monoxide (CO), a colorless, odorless, tasteless gas that is immediately dangerous to life at high concentrations. Operating gasoline-powered equipment indoors without proper ventilation causes rapid CO poisoning and death.

fdny.q: When must a portable gasoline-powered generator be refueled?
choices: While it is running, to avoid a restart
Only when it is shut down and the engine has cooled
At any time — modern generators have spill-proof caps
Only by FDNY-certified personnel
fdny.exp: Portable generators and fueled equipment must be SHUT DOWN and allowed to cool before refueling. Refueling a running or hot engine risks fuel ignition from hot engine surfaces or electrical sparks, causing fire or explosion.

fdny.q: The F-80 Certificate of Fitness is specifically required for:
choices: All residential buildings over 75 feet in height
Coordinators of Fire Safety and Alarm Systems in homeless shelters
Supervisors of fire suppression systems in factories
Fire drill conductors in schools
fdny.exp: The F-80 Certificate of Fitness is for Coordinators of Fire Safety and Alarm Systems in homeless shelters and similar facilities. F-80 holders are responsible for fire safety, alarm systems, and emergency procedures in these occupancies.

fdny.q: In buildings where an F-80 Coordinator of Fire Safety and Alarm Systems is required, who would conduct fire and emergency drills?
choices: An F-07 Fire and Emergency Drill Conductor
The F-80 Coordinator — not an F-07 holder
Any trained staff member
The building owner
fdny.exp: In buildings requiring an F-80 (or F-25/F-58 Fire Safety Director), the F-80 holder is responsible for fire safety duties including staff training and drills. An F-07 holder should NOT be conducting drills in these buildings — the F-80 holder takes that role.

fdny.q: The F-89 Certificate of Fitness is for:
choices: Supervising sprinkler systems in high-rise buildings
Fire and Life Safety Directors in the highest-risk occupancies — high-rise office buildings, hotels, and large assembly occupancies
All building superintendents in NYC
Persons who conduct CPR and first aid training
fdny.exp: The F-89/T-89 is the most comprehensive FDNY Certificate of Fitness — for Fire and Life Safety Directors (FLSD) in high-rise buildings and large occupancies. It requires extensive training (T-89 is the training course) and covers all aspects of fire safety management.

fdny.q: A holder of the F-89/T-89 Certificate of Fitness is exempt from obtaining a separate S-95 Certificate for:
choices: All premises throughout New York City
Only the specific premises listed on their F-89 Certificate of Fitness
No premises — all certificates must be obtained separately
Only premises with fewer than 10 floors
fdny.exp: F-89/T-89 holders are exempt from the S-95 (fire alarm supervision) requirement for the SAME premises listed on their F-89 certificate. For different premises, they may obtain an S-95 without retaking the full exam (employer letter and fee required).

fdny.q: Which FDNY Certificate of Fitness has the MOST comprehensive exam (most questions)?
choices: F-07 (40 questions)
S-95 (35 questions)
S-12 (75 questions)
G-44 (25 questions)
fdny.exp: The S-12 (Citywide Sprinkler System) exam has 75 multiple-choice questions — the most of any FDNY Certificate of Fitness. This reflects the extensive technical knowledge required for sprinkler system supervision.

cna.q: A nurse aide observes a resident has bruising on both arms. What is the FIRST action to take?
choices: Document it in the chart and say nothing
Report the observation immediately to the licensed nurse
Confront the aide who was on the previous shift
Wait to see if more bruising appears before reporting
cna.exp: Nurse aides have a legal obligation to immediately report observations of possible abuse or injury to the supervising licensed nurse. Documentation follows — reporting comes first.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: What is the correct order in the chain of command for a nurse aide?
choices: Nurse aide → doctor → administrator
Nurse aide → licensed nurse → charge nurse
Nurse aide → family member → licensed nurse
Nurse aide → other aides → director of nursing
cna.exp: Nurse aides report directly to the licensed nurse (LPN or RN). The chain is: nurse aide → licensed nurse → charge nurse → supervisor. Never skip a level.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A resident tells a nurse aide, 'I don't want my daughter to know about my health.' The aide should:
choices: Tell the daughter anyway because family has the right to know
Respect the resident's wish — this is protected by HIPAA
Inform the charge nurse that the resident is difficult
Document that the resident is confused
cna.exp: HIPAA protects the resident's right to confidentiality. Residents have the right to control who receives their health information, including family members.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: Which behavior by a nurse aide is an example of resident abuse?
choices: Repositioning a resident every 2 hours
Calling a resident 'sweetie' instead of their name
Withholding a resident's meal because they were uncooperative
Documenting a resident's refusal of medication
cna.exp: Withholding food as punishment is a form of physical abuse and neglect. Using nicknames may be inappropriate but is not abuse; the other options are proper care.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: When documenting care, a nurse aide should ALWAYS:
choices: Write in pencil so corrections can be made easily
Sign with initials only to save time
Record only unusual events — skip routine care
Record accurately and completely, then sign with full name/title
cna.exp: Documentation must be accurate, complete, legible, and signed with the nurse aide's full name and title. Pencil is not acceptable; routine care must be documented.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A nurse aide uses proper body mechanics. This means the aide should:
choices: Bend at the waist to lift a resident
Keep feet together to maintain balance
Bend at the knees and keep the back straight when lifting
Twist the body when transferring a resident
cna.exp: Proper body mechanics: bend knees (not waist), keep back straight, keep load close to body, and avoid twisting. This prevents musculoskeletal injury to both aide and resident.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A resident's family member demands to see the resident's medical chart. The nurse aide should:
choices: Show them the chart — they have the right as family
Refuse and explain only the nurse or doctor can authorize access
Hand them the chart to avoid conflict
Make a copy of the chart for the family
cna.exp: Medical records are confidential. Only the resident or designated authorized persons may access medical records. The aide should direct the family to the charge nurse.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: The Nurse Aide Registry is:
choices: A list of all medications a nurse aide can administer
A state-maintained record of certified nurse aides, including abuse findings
A log of every resident a nurse aide has cared for
A schedule of continuing education requirements
cna.exp: States maintain a Nurse Aide Registry (NAR) listing all certified CNAs and any substantiated findings of abuse, neglect, or misappropriation of property.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: Which action by a nurse aide BEST promotes resident self-determination?
choices: Choosing the resident's clothing to save time
Asking the resident what they want to wear that day
Telling the resident what they will eat at each meal
Bathing the resident at the facility's convenience
cna.exp: Self-determination means residents have the right to make their own choices. Asking about clothing preferences respects autonomy and dignity.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A nurse aide disagrees with a care decision. The BEST response is to:
choices: Refuse to carry out the care plan
Ignore the plan and do what seems best
Express concerns to the charge nurse through proper channels
Complain to other nurse aides
cna.exp: Concerns should be communicated professionally through the chain of command. Refusing care or changing the care plan without authorization is inappropriate.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A nurse aide finds a resident's dentures on the floor. The aide should:
choices: Throw them away if broken
Clean them, label them, and report to the nurse
Give them to the roommate to hold
Leave them on the bedside table without reporting
cna.exp: Residents' personal property must be protected and cared for. The aide should clean the dentures, ensure they're labeled, and report the incident to the nurse.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Role of the Nurse Aide

cna.q: A nurse aide enters a resident's room and smells smoke. The FIRST action is to:
choices: Open the window to clear the air
Pull the fire alarm and call for help immediately
Look for the source of the smoke before alerting anyone
Continue with the scheduled care
cna.exp: RACE: Rescue → Alarm → Confine → Extinguish. The first priority after removing residents in immediate danger is activating the fire alarm.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: A resident tells the nurse aide they feel dizzy when standing up. This is called:
choices: Hypertension
Orthostatic hypotension
Bradycardia
Hypoglycemia
cna.exp: Orthostatic (postural) hypotension is a drop in blood pressure when changing from lying/sitting to standing. Residents should be helped to sit at the edge of the bed before standing.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: Which measure BEST prevents falls for a resident who is unsteady?
choices: Applying a full-body physical restraint
Placing a call light within reach and answering call lights promptly
Putting side rails up on all four sides of the bed
Leaving the resident alone to build confidence
cna.exp: Fall prevention focuses on accessibility, monitoring, and environment. Call light availability and prompt response are key. Restraints require physician orders and carry significant risks.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: A nursing home uses physical restraints. Nurse aides should know that restrained residents must be:
choices: Restrained for no more than 2 hours without being released
Released, repositioned, and toileted at least every 2 hours
Left in restraints all night for safety
Only released by a nurse, not a nurse aide
cna.exp: CMS regulations require restrained residents be released at least every 2 hours for repositioning, exercise, toileting, and circulation checks.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: An ambulatory resident starts to fall while walking. The nurse aide should:
choices: Let the resident fall — it's safer than catching them
Grab the resident's arm and pull them upright
Lower the resident to the floor gently, protecting their head
Yell loudly to startle the resident upright
cna.exp: If a resident begins to fall, guide them down gently to protect from injury. Do not try to hold them upright — this can injure both resident and aide. Protect the head.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: The safest way to identify a resident before providing care is to:
choices: Ask the resident their name
Check the name on the door of their room
Check the resident's ID bracelet and/or photo ID per facility policy
Ask a family member who is visiting
cna.exp: Resident identification must use at least one facility-approved method — typically ID bracelet and/or photo. Asking the name alone is not reliable for confused residents.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: A nurse aide notices a wet floor near the bathroom. The BEST action is:
choices: Walk around it and continue with care
Clean it up immediately or place a wet floor sign and alert housekeeping
Report it in the next shift change
Tell the resident to be careful
cna.exp: Wet floors are an immediate fall hazard. The nurse aide should clean the spill immediately or mark it and alert housekeeping — never ignore or just warn others.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: Which is an example of a restraint alternative?
choices: Wrist restraints to prevent IV removal
Vest (Posey) restraints at night
Bed/chair alarms and increased supervision
Tying the resident loosely to the wheelchair
cna.exp: Restraint alternatives include bed/chair alarms, increased monitoring, positioning devices, and activities. These are preferred before considering physical restraints.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Promotion of Safety

cna.q: A resident is on a dysphagia diet. This means the resident:
choices: Cannot eat any solid food — liquids only
Has difficulty swallowing and needs food and/or liquids modified in texture
Is on a low-sodium diet for heart health
Is diabetic and needs carbohydrate counting
cna.exp: Dysphagia is difficulty swallowing. Dysphagia diets modify food texture and liquid thickness to reduce the risk of aspiration (food entering the airway).
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Function & Health

cna.q: Active range of motion (ROM) exercises are performed:
choices: By the nurse aide moving the resident's joints
By the resident themselves, with or without guidance
Only by a physical therapist
While the resident is sleeping
cna.exp: Active ROM: the resident moves the joints themselves. Passive ROM: the aide moves the joints. Active-assistive ROM: the resident moves with some help. Active ROM builds strength.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Function & Health

cna.q: A resident is incontinent. The nurse aide should:
choices: Scold the resident to encourage control
Change the resident promptly and provide perineal care to prevent skin breakdown
Limit fluids so incontinence occurs less often
Leave the resident in wet clothing during the night shift
cna.exp: Prompt cleaning and perineal care after incontinence prevents skin breakdown and pressure injuries. Limiting fluids can cause dehydration and urinary tract infections.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Function & Health

cna.q: A pressure injury (pressure ulcer) is MOST likely to develop over:
choices: Abdominal tissue
Bony prominences such as the heels, sacrum, and hips
Soft tissue in the upper arms
The forehead
cna.exp: Pressure injuries develop when tissue is compressed between a bony prominence and an external surface. Common sites: sacrum, heels, hips, elbows, and back of head.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Function & Health

cna.q: How often should a bedbound resident be repositioned to prevent pressure injuries?
choices: Once per shift (every 8 hours)
Every 4 hours
At least every 2 hours
Only when the resident requests it
cna.exp: Standard practice is to reposition bedbound residents at least every 2 hours to relieve pressure and maintain skin integrity. More frequent repositioning may be ordered.
cna.source: CNA Certification Exam — Function & Health

cna.q: A resident refuses a bath. The nurse aide should:
